data.title: Superstate completes additional Series B funding round, with Invesco Private Capital, a subsidiary of Invesco, becoming a new investor.
data.description: Fintech company Superstate announced the completion of a Series B funding round, with Invesco Private Capital, a subsidiary of asset management giant Invesco, becoming a new investor. The amount was not disclosed. Simultaneously, Invesco will become the investment manager of Superstate's flagship tokenized fund, USTB (Short-Term US Treasury Bond Fund), starting in Q2 2026. This fund, with approximately $900 million in assets, is one of the world's largest tokenized government bond products. Following the handover, the fund will be renamed, and Superstate will retain its digital transfer agent functions. This move marks Invesco's full-scale entry into the blockchain fund sector. (Prnewswire)

data.title: ECB Vice President: Impact of Iran Conflict on Inflation Depends on Duration
data.description: The European Central Bank's Vice President, Luis de Guindos, has commented on the ongoing conflict involving Iran, stating that its impact on inflation will largely depend on how long the conflict persists. According to Jin10, Guindos emphasized that the duration of the conflict is a critical factor in determining its economic consequences. He noted that prolonged instability could exacerbate inflationary pressures, affecting the broader economy. The situation remains fluid, and the ECB is closely monitoring developments to assess potential impacts on monetary policy and economic stability.

data.title: Crypto Analyst Highlights RAVE Supply Concentration Concerns
data.description: Crypto analyst MLM has raised concerns about the concentration of RAVE token supply, noting that the Ravedao team effectively controls over 98% of the total supply. According to NS3.AI, a presumed team wallet holds 95.3% of the tokens, with additional suspected insider-held exchange wallets contributing to this concentration. MLM suggests that this level of control places RAVE's effective market capitalization at approximately $200 million. Meanwhile, CoinMarketCap lists the token at $9.47, with a fully diluted valuation of about $9.47 billion, following a significant seven-day increase of 3,747.59%.

data.title: Bitcoin Traders Brace for Price Decline Amid Market Uncertainty
data.description:
On April 13, Maxime Seiler, CEO of cryptocurrency trading firm STS Digital, highlighted that Bitcoin traders are preparing for a potential price drop. According to BlockBeats, Seiler's report indicates a significant demand for bearish options over bullish ones, with investors paying premiums for downside protection while selling bets on price increases, reflecting market anxiety.

This bearish sentiment emerges as Bitcoin trades slightly above $70,000. The cryptocurrency experienced a nearly 4% decline last weekend following U.S. President Donald Trump's threat to block the Hormuz Strait.

On Monday, the U.S. Central Command announced that the Navy would intercept all vessels entering and exiting Iranian ports starting at 10 a.m. Eastern Time. Consequently, oil prices surged above $100 per barrel, signaling increased inflationary pressures. Global central bank governors are expected to closely monitor these developments during their meeting in late April. The prices of risk assets like Bitcoin are heavily influenced by these institutions' policies, as they determine the monetary supply within the financial system. The unresolved Middle East conflict remains a key focus for the market this week.

data.title: Circle CEO Clarifies Stance on USDC Wallet Freezes Amid Criticism
data.description: Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire addressed concerns regarding the company's policy on freezing USDC wallets, stating that Circle does not freeze wallets unless they are involved in legal proceedings. According to NS3.AI, this statement comes after criticism emerged over Circle's decision not to freeze $230 million in USDC linked to Drift. Earlier this month, Drift experienced a suspected cyberattack attributed to North Korean hackers, resulting in losses of up to $280 million.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin still has the potential to rise to $88,000, and the overall crypto market remains resilient.
data.description: Bitcoin is currently fluctuating around $70,000, with the overall crypto market showing resilience. Meanwhile, major crypto assets such as Ethereum, XRP, and Solana are also relatively stable. The key in the short term is whether Bitcoin can hold the important support level of $70,000. This level has become a watershed between bulls and bears and has been repeatedly defended by "bottom-fishing" funds. If it breaks down effectively, Bitcoin's price volatility could intensify due to the thin liquidity below. However, if it can stabilize, the market is expected to recover quickly. Some believe that with support from capital flows and macroeconomic factors, Bitcoin still has the potential to rise to $88,000. Overall, although Bitcoin's price performance is stable, speculative behavior combined with negative events may suppress market risk appetite in the short term. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Bitget's Q1 2026 Report Highlights Non-Crypto Asset Trading Volume
data.description: Bitget's Q1 2026 transparency report reveals that non-crypto assets, including commodities, constituted 20%–40% of the trading volume by the end of the first quarter. According to NS3.AI, crypto assets made up 60%–80% of the volume during the same period. The exchange also emphasized its UEX white paper and introduced new AI trading tools.

data.title: STOCKS | Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley Experience Pre-Market Declines
data.description: Goldman Sachs shares fell by 3.7% in pre-market trading, while Morgan Stanley's stock saw a 1.9% decline. According to Jin10, these movements come amid broader market fluctuations and investor concerns over potential economic impacts. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation to assess the implications for the financial sector.

data.title: Hong Kong Stock Consumption ETF Management Fee Reduction Announced
data.description: On April 13, Jin10 reported that the management fee annual rate for the Hong Kong Stock Consumption ETF managed by Yin Hua (159735) will be reduced from 0.50% to 0.15%. Additionally, the fund custody fee annual rate will be lowered from 0.10% to 0.05%. These changes will take effect starting April 14, 2026, inclusive.

data.title: Netanyahu Supports Trump's Iran Containment Policy
data.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has expressed his support for U.S. President Donald Trump's approach to containing Iran. According to NS3.AI, Netanyahu's endorsement aligns with the ongoing efforts by the U.S. administration to address concerns over Iran's influence in the region. This support underscores the close diplomatic ties between Israel and the United States, particularly in matters related to Middle Eastern security and stability.

data.title: Goldman Sachs U.S. Stock Price Reverses Early Gains
data.description: Goldman Sachs experienced a reversal in its U.S. stock price, which initially rose but later declined. According to Jin10, the stock's early gains were not sustained, leading to a downturn in its market performance. This shift reflects broader market volatility and investor sentiment, impacting the financial sector's outlook.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Sees 19% Rise in First Quarter Profit
data.description: Goldman Sachs has announced a significant 19% increase in its first-quarter profit, reflecting strong performance across various sectors. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that the financial giant attributed this growth to robust trading activities and increased investment banking revenues. The bank's strategic focus on expanding its consumer banking division also contributed to the positive results. Analysts noted that Goldman Sachs' ability to navigate market volatility and capitalize on emerging opportunities has been key to its success. The firm's leadership expressed optimism about sustaining this momentum throughout the year, despite potential economic challenges. Goldman Sachs continues to leverage its global presence and diverse portfolio to drive growth and enhance shareholder value.

data.title: Bitget Q1 Transparency Report: Non-crypto asset trading volume rises to 40%, cross-asset allocation trend accelerates.
data.description: Bitget's Q1 2026 Transparency Report, released today, shows that user trading behavior is rapidly shifting from single crypto assets to multi-asset portfolios. By the end of Q1, the trading volume of non-crypto assets such as commodities accounted for 20%-40%, while the share of crypto assets fell from their dominant position at the beginning of the year to 60%-80%. This fluctuation indicates that panoramic asset portfolios have become a core strategy for high-net-worth investors. At the product and ecosystem level, Bitget released the UEX white paper for its panoramic exchange in Q1, further clarifying the integration direction of crypto assets, tokenized assets, and AI-driven trading under a unified architecture. Simultaneously, the platform continues to strengthen its AI trading infrastructure, launching Agent Hub and GetClaw to drive the evolution of AI from an auxiliary tool to an execution system, enabling intelligent agents to access real-time market data, identify trading signals, and autonomously execute operations under given parameters. Bitget CEO Gracy Chen pointed out: "The boundaries between the crypto market and traditional finance are rapidly blurring, and the explosive growth of CFD business foreshadows the arrival of a unified market. Users are no longer making binary asset choices, but rather placing multiple assets within the same logical framework for efficiency competition."

data.title: Hyperliquid mainnet has launched a "priority fee" mechanism, allowing users to pay HYPE to prioritize reading data or writing orders.
data.description: Jeff, the founder of Hyperliquid, announced on Discord that the Priority Fees mechanism is now live on the mainnet in Alpha mode. This includes Gossip Priority Fees (for prioritizing data reads) and Order Priority Fees (for prioritizing order writes), details of which can be found in the official documentation. It's also worth noting that, based on user feedback, the upper limit for Order Priority Fees has been reduced from 20 bps to 8 bps. Currently, Order Priority Fees only apply to IOC orders on HIP-3 assets.

data.title: Hyperliquid Launches Priority Fees Mechanism in Alpha Mode
data.description:
Hyperliquid founder Jeff announced on Discord that the Priority Fees mechanism is now live on the mainnet in Alpha mode. According to Odaily, this includes Gossip Priority Fees for prioritizing data reading and Order Priority Fees for prioritizing order writing. More details can be found in the official documentation.

Additionally, based on user feedback, the cap for Order Priority Fees has been reduced from 20 basis points to 8 basis points. Currently, Order Priority Fees apply only to IOC orders on HIP-3 assets.

data.title: Pershing Square Initiates IPO for U.S. Company Merger
data.description: Pershing Square has announced the launch of an initial public offering (IPO) for the merger of a U.S. company. According to Jin10, the IPO is set with a share price of $50.00 and aims to raise at least $5 billion.

data.title: India Faces Below-Average Monsoon Amid Rising Farmer Challenges
data.description: India is expected to experience a below-average monsoon this year, which could exacerbate the difficulties faced by farmers. Bloomberg posted on X that the agricultural sector is already grappling with increased input costs due to the ongoing Middle East conflict. This weather forecast adds to the challenges for farmers who rely heavily on monsoon rains for their crops. The situation could potentially impact agricultural output and economic stability in the region.

data.title: Elon Musk Assembles Team for Ambitious Chip Plant Project
data.description: Elon Musk is reportedly assembling a team to realize his vision of establishing a large-scale chip manufacturing facility. Bloomberg posted on X that Musk's initiative aims to address the growing demand for advanced semiconductor chips, which are crucial for various technological applications. The move comes amid global efforts to enhance chip production capabilities and reduce reliance on existing supply chains. Musk's plan is expected to leverage cutting-edge technology and innovation to create a state-of-the-art facility. Details regarding the location and timeline for the project remain undisclosed, but industry experts anticipate significant impacts on the semiconductor market.

data.title: Paris Blockchain Week 2026 to Feature High-Level French Officials and Global Executives
data.description: Paris Blockchain Week 2026 is set to take place on April 15 and 16 at the Carrousel du Louvre, featuring prominent French ministers, an ambassador, and nearly 20 Members of Parliament. According to NS3.AI, the conference anticipates attendees from over 100 countries, including top executives from major financial institutions such as BNP Paribas, HSBC, JPMorgan Chase, Goldman Sachs, and Morgan Stanley. The event aims to foster discussions on blockchain technology and its impact on global finance.

data.title: U.S. Court Allows White House Banquet Hall Project to Proceed Temporarily
data.description:
A U.S. federal appeals court has issued a temporary ruling allowing the construction of a White House banquet hall to continue for at least another week. According to BlockBeats, the decision was made by the U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit on Saturday, following a 2-1 vote by the panel. 

The ruling temporarily halts a previous order by Judge Richard Leon, appointed by George W. Bush, who had stopped the project in March. Judge Leon had ruled that the U.S. government lacked congressional authorization to proceed with the construction.
